---
title: "Larnix"
subtitle: "A free, beginner-to-frontier school for AI/ML — where every chapter runs."
---

## What this is

Larnix is a free, open-access course that takes a complete beginner — on a
low-spec laptop, at zero cost — from "what is AI" to building and shipping real
machine-learning systems. It follows the Zerodha Varsity teaching style: plain
language, one idea per chapter, a worked example you can run, a short
"Key Takeaways" box, and graded practice.

This page is the scaffold's hello-world. It exists to prove the site builds from
a fresh clone and renders. Real content arrives later in Phase P0 with the first
sample chapter.

## How the platform is built

- **Read it** as a static website (this Quarto site).
- **Run it** in your browser — beginner chapters execute Python with zero
  install (wired in a later P0 task).
- **Practice it** — exercises are auto-graded in the browser; quizzes score
  client-side.

See the [About](about.qmd) page for the project's goals and the build roadmap.

::: {.callout-note}
This is a development scaffold. The design system, in-browser runtime,
auto-grader, quiz engine, and the first chapter land in subsequent P0 tasks.
:::
